name: TanStack Start
description: Full-stack React framework built on TanStack Router, SSR + streaming + server functions

name.comment: Official name; primary React package is @tanstack/react-start.

description.value: A full-stack React framework powered by TanStack Router, with full-document SSR, streaming, server functions, and unified client/server builds on Vite or Rsbuild

repository.comment: Developed in the TanStack/router monorepo alongside TanStack Router; no separate repository.

license.comment: MIT per the @tanstack/react-start package metadata; docs state it is 100% open source and free to use.

category.comment: Full-stack meta-framework that owns the build/server (Vite or Rsbuild) with routing as one part; built on top of the standalone TanStack Router.

initial-release.comment: First shown publicly as an early pre-release at Compose 2024; reached a v1.0 Release Candidate on 2025-09-23 (still RC, pre-1.0, as of 2026-07-15).

latest-release.comment: @tanstack/react-start@1.168.28, published 2026-07-13 (checked 2026-07-15). Still a v1.0 Release Candidate line, not yet a final 1.0.

github-stars.comment: ~14.8k stars on the shared TanStack/router monorepo, which hosts both TanStack Router and TanStack Start (checked 2026-07-15).

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Weekly downloads of @tanstack/react-start for the week of 2026-07-08 to 2026-07-14 per the npm registry API.

routing-paradigm.comment: Type-safe file-based routing powered by TanStack Router; the underlying code-based (createRoute config object) routing can also be used.

nested-layout-routes.comment: Inherits TanStack Router's nested and pathless/layout routes.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Splat/wildcard and optional path segments via the underlying TanStack Router.

search-params-api.comment: First-class URL-as-state search params with runtime validation and full type safety, inherited from TanStack Router.

route-guards-middleware.comment: Router beforeLoad guards plus server-function/route middleware and context in Start.

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript with first-class type definitions; end-to-end type safety is a headline goal.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Link/navigate targets checked against the known route tree via TanStack Router.

type-safe-search-params.comment: Schema-validated, fully typed search params inherited from TanStack Router.

codegen-required.comment: Type safety is inference-based. File-based routing generates a routeTree.gen.ts via the Vite/Rsbuild plugin for convenience, but the types themselves come from inference rather than codegen.

type-safety-rating.comment: End-to-end inferred type safety across paths, params, search params, loaders, and isomorphic server functions (guideline level 5).

route-data-loaders.comment: Route loaders via TanStack Router, with SSR/hydration handled by Start.

actions-mutations.comment: Isomorphic, type-safe server functions plus server/API routes provide a built-in write/mutation primitive (unlike the standalone router).

prefetching.comment: Automatic route prefetching on intent, plus deep TanStack Query integration for prefetching/caching.

pending-transition-ui.comment: Pending components and React transition integration inherited from TanStack Router.

scroll-restoration.comment: Built-in scroll restoration via TanStack Router (the 2026-07-13 release refined window/element scroll restoration).

per-route-code-splitting.comment: Per-route lazy loading / code splitting via TanStack Router plus Start's full-stack Vite builds.

per-route-error-handling.comment: Route-scoped error boundaries / errorComponent inherited from TanStack Router.

ssr-support.comment: Turnkey full-document server-side rendering is a core, built-in feature of Start.

react-server-components.comment: Not yet a stable feature: an experimental @tanstack/react-start-rsc package exists and docs mention RSC experimentally, but the official v1.0 RC announcement states RSC is in active development and will land as a non-breaking v1.x addition.

static-site-generation.comment: Static prerendering at build time (prerender option in the tanstackStart plugin) produces static HTML for selected routes; also supports SPA mode and ISR.

client-only-usable.comment: Supports a client-only SPA mode / fully static output with no running server, but still requires Start's own Vite/Rsbuild toolchain (it is not a drop-in router).

framework-bound.comment: Meta-framework that requires its own build toolchain (Vite or Rsbuild) and server runtime; cannot be dropped into an arbitrary React app the way the standalone TanStack Router can.

bundle-size.comment: Null per RESEARCH.md: as a meta-framework a single 'router bundle' figure isn't meaningful; Start ships a full client/server runtime rather than a drop-in router package.

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: As a full framework it ships a server runtime and multiple runtime dependencies (e.g. pathe plus several first-party @tanstack/start-* packages).

tree-shakeable.comment: ESM packages built with Vite/Rsbuild; application bundles are tree-shaken during the build.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive guides and API reference (routing, SSR, server functions, prerendering, ISR), but still pre-1.0 with docs polish explicitly noted as pending in the v1 RC announcement (guideline level 4).

official-devtools.comment: Uses the official TanStack Router Devtools for inspecting routes and navigation; part of the same TanStack ecosystem/monorepo.

data-fetching-integration.comment: Built-in route loaders and isomorphic server functions, plus deep first-class TanStack Query (React Query) integration for prefetching/caching/hydration. RSC-based data fetching exists only experimentally, so 'rsc' is omitted for now.

predecessor-lineage.value: Built on TanStack Router (successor to React Location)
predecessor-lineage.comment: Start is the full-stack framework layered on top of TanStack Router, which itself evolved from the React Location project.
